title: ffi/identity: save_identity_to_file writes raw key material with no permissions hardening
description: ffi/src/api/identity.rs:50-64: save_identity_to_file writes 64 raw key bytes to a file using std::fs::write, which on Linux creates the file with umask-derived permissions (typically 0644). On Android the OS sandbox provides isolation, but if deployed on Linux the key file is world-readable. The other keyfile paths (nie-core/keyfile.rs) should be checked for the same issue. For FFI specifically: since the primary target is Android, the OS sandbox makes this acceptable, but it should be documented. A note in the function doc that this relies on OS-level isolation (Android filesDir sandbox) would prevent a future contributor from using this function in a non-sandboxed context and assuming it's secure.
